evals.expected_output: Asks for profile name, asks dev vs production, explains M2M credentials, collects client_id and client_secret separately, calls OAuth token endpoint, writes token and env=development to ~/.config/cyoda/cyoda-plugin-config.json under the selected profile, confirms success without production warning

assertions.text: Asks which profile name to save the token under before proceeding
Asks whether this is development or production environment
Explains that client_id/client_secret are machine-to-machine credentials identifying the application, not a personal login
Asks whether user already has client_id and client_secret before collecting

Asks whether user already has credentials — if the user says no, would direct them to Cyoda AI Studio at https://ai.cyoda.net/; in this eval the user says yes so the redirect path is not exercised but the conditional check must be present
Collects client_id and client_secret separately, one at a time
Calls OAuth token endpoint
Writes token and env=development to ~/.config/cyoda/cyoda-plugin-config.json under the selected profile
Does NOT echo client_secret back in the conversation
Does NOT show production warning for development login
Does NOT write any .gitignore entry

evals.prompt: /cyoda:auth — production environment, I accept the risk
evals.expected_output: Asks profile name, shows security warning, requires explicit 'yes', explains M2M credentials, collects credentials, writes token and env=production to ~/.config/cyoda/cyoda-plugin-config.json under the selected profile, shows prominent production reminder

assertions.text: Asks which profile name to save the token under
Shows security warning about storing production credentials on disk
Requires explicit 'yes' — not any other response
Shows the security warning and asks yes/no as a distinct step even though the user pre-stated acceptance in the prompt — does NOT skip the confirmation
Explains that client_id/client_secret are M2M credentials for the application
Writes env=production to ~/.config/cyoda/cyoda-plugin-config.json after confirmation
Shows prominent production reminder after successful login
Does NOT write any .gitignore entry

evals.prompt: /cyoda:auth — production, but I'm not sure about storing credentials
evals.expected_output: Shows security warning, stops when user does not answer 'yes', does not write any credentials, suggests alternatives

assertions.text: Shows security warning before proceeding
Stops when user response is not explicit 'yes'
Does NOT write any credentials after a non-yes response
Suggests CYODA_TOKEN env var as an alternative to persisting credentials on disk

evals.prompt: /cyoda:auth — my login stopped working after the environment was redeployed
evals.expected_output: Recognises the post-redeploy scenario, notes that technical users may be deleted on redeploy, directs user to recreate the technical user in AI Studio before retrying

assertions.text: Recognises the post-redeploy scenario as cause of credential failure
Notes that technical users may be deleted when an environment is redeployed
Directs user to recreate the technical user in Cyoda AI Studio at https://ai.cyoda.net/
After user provides new credentials, completes the full auth flow — calls token endpoint and writes token to ~/.config/cyoda/cyoda-plugin-config.json

evals.prompt: /cyoda:auth — connect to a Cyoda Cloud dev environment with client_id=abc123 and client_secret=xyz789
evals.expected_output: Uses Authorization: Basic header with base64-encoded client_id:client_secret against /api/oauth/token on the first attempt, without iterating through multiple paths

assertions.text: Uses Authorization: Basic header with base64-encoded client_id:client_secret — credentials are NOT in the request body
Calls /api/oauth/token on the first attempt — does NOT try /oauth/token, /auth/token, or other paths first
Does NOT iterate through multiple token endpoint paths on failure — consults cyoda help config auth instead
If token call fails, runs 'cyoda help config auth' before retrying — does not guess alternate paths

evals.expected_output: Calls /api/oauth/token, gets a 4xx error, runs 'cyoda help config auth' to get the correct endpoint, does NOT try alternate paths like /oauth/token or /auth/token, retries with the corrected endpoint from cyoda help output

assertions.text: Calls /api/oauth/token on the first attempt before any fallback
After receiving a 4xx error, runs 'cyoda help config auth' before attempting any retry
Does NOT try /oauth/token, /auth/token, or any other alternate paths — only retries after reading cyoda help output
Retries the token call using the endpoint path from cyoda help output, not a guessed path
